title: WOS API Appeal
description: Public API resource schema for WOS appeals. Appeals are cross-case governance artifacts: an appellant challenges an adverse determination on a closed workflow process, triggering a governed review process. The `appeal` entity-type literal is already admitted in the URN alternation at `_common.schema.json#/$defs/WosResourceUrn` (Phase 1). Actor references are `ActorRef` URNs per ADR 0082 D-9; URNs follow the closed `WosResourceUrn` taxonomy per ADR 0082 D-4. Closed taxonomies (status, disposition) per D-12; vendor extensions are NOT permitted — appeal lifecycle and disposition are normative.

ActorRef.description: Stable actor reference from the identity/governance subsystem (ADR 0082 D-9). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json` per PLN-0404.

WosResourceUrn.description: Public WOS API resource URN (ADR 0082 D-4). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json` per PLN-0404.

Appeal.description: Appeal resource against a closed workflow process's adverse determination (governance S3.5). An appeal is filed by an appellant actor, reviewed by an adjudicator, and terminates with a closed disposition. This shape is the cross-case authoritative record; per-case projections on `WorkflowProcessGovernance` (instance.schema.json) summarize the appeal state for the consuming instance.

id.description: Stable public URN for this appeal. Entity-type segment MUST be `appeal`.

processId.description: Workflow process URN this appeal was filed against. Entity-type segment MUST be `instance`.

appellantRef.description: URN-form reference to the appellant actor.

filedAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when the appeal was filed.

groundForAppeal.description: Free-text ground for appeal. Open prose.

disposition.description: Closed disposition. Present only when status is accepted or denied.

dispositionAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when the disposition was recorded. Present only when disposition is present.

adjudicatorRef.description: URN-form reference to the adjudicator reviewing the appeal.

createdAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when this appeal resource was created.

AppealCreateRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/instances/{id}/appeals` — files an appeal against a closed workflow process's adverse determination. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED per ADR 0082 D-16; a repeat request within the retention window returns the original `Appeal` resource unchanged. Server assigns the URN, `filedAt`, and `createdAt`; clients MUST NOT supply them.

processId.description: Workflow process URN to file the appeal against. Entity-type segment MUST be `instance`. Server validates the instance is in a completed lifecycle state.

appellantRef.description: URN-form reference to the appellant actor filing the appeal. Server verifies the appellant has standing to appeal this instance.

groundForAppeal.description: Free-text ground for appeal. Open prose.

processId.description: When present, restrict results to appeals for this workflow process.

status.description: When present, restrict results to appeals in this lifecycle posture.

cursor.description: Opaque resume token returned by the previous page (ADR 0082 D-7).

limit.description: Maximum number of appeals requested for this page (ADR 0082 D-7).

AppealPage.description: Cursor-paginated appeal page (ADR 0082 D-7). Default ordering is `createdAt` ascending so paginating clients observe a stable monotone walk.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.
